assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 1: 'Step 7 -- Concurrent Triage Detection for TC-8020'. Lines 7: upstream component is quinn-proto. Lines 23-27: search returned 1 result, table shows TC-8019 with status 'In Progress'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 25-27: table under 'Search Results' shows '| TC-8019 | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com |'. Lines 29-33: 'Concurrent Triage Warning' section references both TC-8019 and 'engineer-b@example.com'.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 55-70: 'Options Presented to Engineer' section lists: '1. Wait -- pause until the other triage completes', '2. Skip -- skip remediation task creation for this CVE', '3. Proceed -- create tasks anyway with a `concurrent-triage-overlap` label'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md line 42: 'Step 7 -- Concurrent Triage Detection: BLOCKED' appears before the triage decision sections. Line 50-51: 'The engineer must choose one of three options before remediation tasks can be created'. Case A discussion begins at line 62: 'Case A applies: Affected -- create remediation tasks' under 'Triage Outcome (pending Step 7 resolution)' at line 59.
